classObjective: Analyze boundary lines, half-planes, and test points to determine solution regions and verify whether ordered pairs satisfy a two-variable inequality.
workedExamples: 6-5, Example 3 — Write and Graph Inequalities from Context (continued)
6-5, Example 4 — Graph Special-Case Inequalities (Horizontal and Vertical Lines)
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: test point (0, 0) when not on boundary; feasible region identification; special case horizontal/vertical lines
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice analyzing half-planes and test points to verify solutions to two-variable inequalities
independentPractice: Practice problems determining solution regions and verifying whether ordered pairs satisfy two-variable inequalities
capReflection: Why is (0, 0) often the easiest test point to use, and when should you avoid it?
